af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Dead-simple personal push notification API. Application token + user key — both required per request but extremely simple to use. Excellent documentation. No delivery receipts unless using Emergency priority (2) with callbacks. Monthly message limits per application token. HTML formatting supported with html=1 parameter. No two-way messaging. One-time purchase model.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced for push notification delivery. Application token + user key authentication — simple but functional. No fine-grained scope control. Simple, maintained service with minimal attack surface. API tokens via environment variables. No sensitive data processed beyond notification content.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Very stable long-running service. One-time purchase model aligns incentives with reliability. Stable simple API with no breaking changes over years. Emergency priority (2) callbacks provide delivery confirmation. Retry guidance not formally documented. Monthly quota limits need tracking.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. IMPORTANT: This is an MCP tool CONSUMER (embedded in the voice client), not an MCP server. Voice AI client ported from xiaozhi-esp32 — not an API that agents can call. Device activation + captcha auth against Xiaozhi Chinese cloud servers. Python 3.9-3.12 only (strict version requirement). WebRTC audio setup can be complex. Data residency in China is a significant concern for non-Chinese users. Limited to Xiaozhi ecosystem.

security_score_components.security_notes: All voice AI processing goes through Xiaozhi cloud servers in China — significant data residency concern. Non-standard device activation + captcha auth. No fine-grained access control. Community port project — verify all dependencies. Credentials managed via config files. GDPR/privacy compliance uncertain for non-Chinese users.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Depends on Xiaozhi cloud server availability in China. No SLA. Early port project with limited reliability track record. Python version restrictions may cause compatibility issues. WebRTC audio can be unreliable on some platforms. Not suitable for production use cases.

af_score_components.notes: Official MCP server with qdrant-store and qdrant-find tools. High-performance open-source vector database in Rust with REST + gRPC APIs, excellent docs, and SDKs in 6 languages. API key auth with read-only/full-access distinction. Self-hosted has no auth by default — must be explicitly configured. Collection must be created with correct vector dimensionality before insertion. gRPC is significantly faster for bulk upserts. Payload indexes required for filtered search performance.

security_score_components.security_notes: Qdrant Cloud: HTTPS enforced. Self-hosted: TLS is operator responsibility; no auth by default (must configure). API key via X-API-Key header. Read-only vs full-access key types available. SOC2, GDPR, ISO27001 for cloud version. Open source Rust codebase — auditable. No RBAC beyond read/write split.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Qdrant Cloud has status page and SLA. SOC2/ISO27001 certified. Stable REST and gRPC APIs. Retry guidance documented. Cursor-based pagination. Quantization and sparse vector config locked at collection creation — cannot change without recreating. MCP server's two-tool interface covers agent memory use case well.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. NASDAQ Data Link (formerly Quandl) — financial and economic dataset API. Simple single API key. Free tier limited to 50 requests/day — very restrictive for agents. Quandl→NASDAQ Data Link rebrand means many tutorials have outdated URLs. Python SDK still named 'quandl' for legacy compatibility. Dataset availability varies — some historical datasets retired. Premium datasets require separate provider agreements.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Single API key per account — no fine-grained scopes. NASDAQ-owned infrastructure with enterprise reliability. No sensitive personal data handled — financial market data only. API key via environment variables. Free vs premium dataset access distinguished by account subscription, not key scopes.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: NASDAQ-backed infrastructure. Quandl rebrand to NASDAQ Data Link caused URL and SDK changes. Some historical datasets have been retired without notice. Date format and column names vary significantly across datasets. Retry guidance not formally documented. Offset-based pagination.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Dominant small business accounting API. auth_complexity_score=30 (very complex) — OAuth2 MANDATORY with no API key option; requires user authorization flow; access tokens expire in 1 hour; refresh tokens expire in 100 days. Hard for autonomous agents. SyncToken required on all updates — must fetch current value before writes. DELETE not supported — use void/archive. Minor version parameter can break compatibility. Aggressive rate limiting during tax season.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. OAuth2 with accounting and payment scopes — strong security via user consent model. SOC2 Type II, PCI-DSS, GDPR compliant. OAuth token management (1-hour expiry) requires secure token storage and refresh logic. Secrets (client ID/secret) via environment variables. Sandbox/production company ID separation.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SOC2 with status page. Minor version parameter creates compatibility complexity. SyncToken requirement means concurrent writes fail. Aggressive rate limiting during tax season impacts agent workflows. No retry guidance documented. Custom SQL-like query language has significant limitations. Offset-based pagination.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Open-source AMQP message broker with Management HTTP API. IMPORTANT: Management REST API is for administration only — actual message operations use AMQP protocol with client libraries (pika for Python, amqplib for Node). Channel errors close the channel but not connection — agents must re-open after errors. Consumer acknowledgement patterns critical for correctness. Queue and exchange topology must be declared before use. Virtual hosts provide logical isolation.

security_score_components.security_notes: Self-hosted — TLS needs explicit configuration for both AMQP and Management HTTP. HTTP Basic Auth for management API, SASL for AMQP. Virtual host isolation provides tenant separation. User-level resource permissions per vhost. OAuth2 plugin available for enterprise setups. Apache-licensed, actively maintained. Passwords/credentials via config/env vars.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted — reliability depends on operator infrastructure (SOC2 for hosted CloudAMQP). Stable AMQP 0-9-1 protocol with decades of production use. Retry guidance documented. Message acknowledgement patterns prevent loss. Clustering for HA adds operational complexity. Consumer redelivery on channel errors needs correct handling.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Location intelligence platform with geofencing, trip tracking, geocoding, and place detection. Publishable key for client-side SDK (can be exposed), secret key for server-side API (must be private). Test and live environments with separate key pairs. Geofencing events require mobile SDK for device-side detection — server-side geofence checks use different endpoint. Trip tracking state machine (started → approaching → arrived) requires understanding before implementation.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Publishable/secret key split is a security best practice — client-safe key vs server-only key. Test/live environment isolation. SOC2 Type II, GDPR, CCPA compliant. Secret key via environment variables. Location data is sensitive PII — handled with appropriate compliance framework.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Status page, SOC2. Stable API with OpenAPI spec. Retry guidance documented. Cursor-based pagination. Webhooks require HTTPS endpoint. Radar places database differs from Foursquare — categories don't map 1:1. Test/live environment separation requires care in deployment.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Personal reading highlights API for Kindle, Instapaper, Pocket, articles, books. Simple token-based auth (Authorization: Token header). Highlights API and Reader API are separate systems with different base URLs. Rate limits are undocumented — agents must implement conservative throttling. No webhooks — polling required for new highlights. Full text of articles not available — highlights only. Tags are user-defined and inconsistent.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Personal access token authentication with OAuth2 option. GDPR compliant. No fine-grained scope control — full account access per token. Consumer service handling personal reading data. Token via Authorization header/env vars.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Consumer subscription service with no formal SLA. Highlights API and Reader API are separate systems requiring separate integration. No webhooks — polling only. Rate limits undocumented — unpredictable throttling. Book/article metadata completeness varies by source. Generally reliable for personal use but not production-grade.

af_score_components.notes: Has MCP server with 29 tools for AI assistant integration. PostgreSQL-backed Reddit archive tool from Pushshift dumps. DATABASE_URL env var for PostgreSQL — no API auth layer on top. 29 MCP tools may create tool-selection confusion for agents. Requires downloading multi-hundred-GB Pushshift dumps before any queries work. GIN index creation on large datasets takes hours to days. Tor-optimized design. Default passwords need manual changing.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local REST API without documented TLS. No authentication layer on the API — any local process can query. DATABASE_URL via env var (correct). Default database passwords documented as needing change — security risk if ignored. Small community project — dependency audit required. Data may include deleted/removed Reddit content — verify legal compliance for jurisdiction.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted offline tool — no SLA. Requires massive upfront data import (hundreds of GB). Index creation is time-intensive. Small community project with limited production track record. Cursor-based pagination. Once set up, query reliability depends on PostgreSQL deployment quality.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Reddit REST API. OAuth2 required — script apps use password grant for bots, web apps use authorization code flow, app-only auth for read-only. User-Agent header must be in exact format 'platform:app_id:version (by /u/username)' or requests are rejected. OAuth token expires in 1 hour — refresh needed for long-running agents. Pagination uses 'after' cursor (fullname of last item). Rate limits apply per OAuth client — multi-user bots hit limits faster. Major API pricing changes in 2023 impacted many third-party apps.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. OAuth2 with granular scopes (read, write, modposts, identity, etc.). GDPR/CCPA compliant. 2023 API changes affected reliability and trust of the platform. Client credentials via environment variables. OAuth token management requires secure storage.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: 2023 API pricing changes caused significant disruption to third-party apps and bots. Subreddit content policies vary — what works in one sub fails in another. No retry guidance documented. Non-standard cursor pagination requires parsing from response. Rate limits per OAuth client create fleet-scale concerns. Platform reliability generally good but trust has been impacted.
